attractions.name: Linh Ung Pagoda – Son Tra

attractions.name: Bai Bac Beach
attractions.description: A hidden gem often missed by tour groups. White sands, clear waters, and few crowds—ideal for swimming or just lounging

local_dishes.name: Ramie leaf cake (Banh it la gai)
Mi Quang
local_dishes.description: Turmeric noodles with shrimp/pork and crispy rice crackers - a Da Nang specialty

local_dishes.name: Phở
local_dishes.description: The quintessential Vietnamese dish, flat rice noodles with boiled chicken in a hearty stock

local_dishes.name: Banh Mi
local_dishes.description: Paté and margarine are spread across the soft, chewy interior of a baguette and loaded with pickled vegetables, fresh cilantro and cucumber

local_dishes.name: Bun Cha Ca
local_dishes.description: Fish cake noodle soup popular in Central Vietnam

local_dishes.name: Rau Muong Xao Toi
local_dishes.description: Made by picking out fresh water spinach and frying it with garlic and other Vietnamese herbs and served with a topping of peanut powder

local_dishes.name: Banh Mi Chay
local_dishes.description: Vegetarian version of the famous Vietnamese snack Banh Mi. French style baguette sandwich filled with tofu, cheese or fried eggs

local_dishes.name: Egg coffee
local_dishes.description: The creamy soft, meringue-like egg white foam perched on the dense Vietnamese coffee- is more dessert like

safety_tips.tip: Avoid nighttime travel in remote or poorly lit areas, especially outside Hoi An or Son Tra
Avoid beaches after dark – avoid isolated areas
Be careful with motorbike traffic – always use pedestrian crossings carefully
Watch out for taxi meter rigging – Always use trusted apps like Grab for safe pricing
Be aware of currency confusion – VND has high denominations; double-check notes before handing over
Don't carry all your cash/cards in one place
Use hotel safes for passports and valuables
Be cautious at night markets; petty theft is rare but possible
Carry a copy of your passport when exploring
